Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows evidence of renal impairment (elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria), hypoglycemia (low glucose, low HbA1c), and mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though diabetes control appears excessive. No signs of urinary infection. Recommend urgent clinical correlation for renal function, hypoglycemia risk, and further nephrology evaluation.
